meta.title: Ransomware — Finance file server (BlackCat/ALPHV)
meta.narrative: At approximately 06:42 on 28 June, the SOC received multiple EDR alerts for mass file encryption activity on FIN-SRV-02, the primary finance file server. Ransom notes (RECOVER-FILES.txt) were dropped across accessible shares. Initial triage indicates BlackCat/ALPHV tradecraft. Entry vector appears to be a compromised VPN account (no MFA) belonging to a contractor, first used from an anomalous ASN at 02:14. Lateral movement to FIN-SRV-02 followed via RDP using harvested domain credentials. Approximately 340GB of finance data was staged and exfiltrated to an external host prior to encryption. Containment achieved by 11:20 through network isolation of affected segment and disabling of the compromised accounts.

govern.assets: FIN-SRV-02 (finance file server), FIN-SRV-01 (finance app server), VPN concentrator, DC-01 (domain controller — credential exposure suspected)
govern.dataTypes: Employee PII, payroll records, supplier bank details, financial statements
govern.legalNotes: Privilege asserted over IR investigation via external counsel. Do not circulate technical findings outside the response team without legal review.
govern.secureComms: Signal group 'IR-014 Bridge'; encrypted email list ir014@
govern.bizImpact: Finance operations halted ~2 days. Payroll run at risk. Estimated direct cost £180k-£240k including IR retainer, overtime, and restoration.
govern.repRisk: High — supplier bank details exposed; potential media interest if data surfaces on leak site.
govern.insurance: Cyber policy CYB-88213 (Beazley). Notified 28 June. Excess £50k.
govern.riskNotes: Threat actor has a known data-leak site. 72h countdown on ransom note before threatened publication.

custody.notes: Imaged with Tableau write-blocker; hash verified against source.
Copied to evidence NAS (encrypted). Original image sealed in evidence locker E-14.
Captured with WinPMEM prior to isolation to preserve volatile artefacts.
Handed working copy to external IR for malware analysis under NDA/privilege.

iocs.notes: Source IP of anomalous VPN login on contractor account (no MFA). Known Tor exit / hosting ASN.
Compromised contractor VPN account used for initial access. Valid credentials, no MFA enforced.
BlackCat/ALPHV encryptor binary (bcrypt.exe). Matches known ALPHV sample family.
External host receiving ~340GB outbound over HTTPS prior to encryption. Data staging/exfil endpoint.
C2 / exfil staging domain resolved by FIN-SRV-02. Registered 3 days before incident.
Ransom note dropped in every encrypted directory. Contains .onion leak-site URL and 72h deadline.
Suspected credential-dumping tool (Mimikatz-like). Detected in memory capture.

actions.title: Isolate FIN-SRV-02 and affected finance segment
actions.notes: Network isolation applied at switch. Segment VLAN quarantined.

actions.title: Disable compromised accounts (svc-contractor01 + reset exposed domain creds)
actions.notes: Contractor account disabled; forced reset of 40 potentially exposed domain accounts.

actions.title: Rebuild FIN-SRV-02 from clean backup and validate
actions.notes: Restoring from 26 June backup (pre-compromise). Validation checklist to follow before return to prod.

actions.title: Enforce MFA on all VPN accounts (close entry vector)
actions.notes: MFA now mandatory on VPN. Contractor access policy under review.

actions.title: Twice-daily executive situation briefing
actions.notes: Ongoing while incident is live. 09:00 and 16:00.

actions.title: Monitor threat-actor leak site for data publication
actions.notes: Check .onion leak site daily for signs of exfiltrated data being posted.

escalations.reason: Confirmed data exfiltration (~340GB finance data incl. PII) prior to encryption — elevates from high to critical given regulatory and reputational exposure.

notifications.basis: Art. 33 UK GDPR — 72h from awareness of breach
notifications.notes: Personal data breach affecting employee and supplier PII. Initial notification filed.
Verbal briefing at 07:20, formal escalation follow-up.

notifications.basis: Preserve legal privilege — notify before external comms
Policy obligation — notify within 24h of major incident
notifications.notes: Claim reference CYB-88213 opened. Mandiant engaged via panel.
Pending legal sign-off on wording before sending.

rootCauses.detail: Contractor VPN account permitted single-factor authentication, allowing use of stolen credentials from an anomalous location.
rootCauses.remediation: MFA now enforced on all remote access. Contractor accounts moved to time-boxed, MFA-mandatory access with quarterly review.

rootCauses.detail: Once on the VPN segment, RDP to the finance server was unrestricted, enabling rapid lateral movement with harvested credentials.
rootCauses.remediation: Network segmentation project prioritised; RDP restricted to jump hosts with MFA and logging.

rootCauses.detail: Large outbound transfer (~340GB) was not alerted on in near-real-time; discovered during post-incident netflow review.
rootCauses.remediation: DLP egress monitoring and volume-based alerting to be implemented on perimeter.

notes.cl10: Rebuild in progress on FIN-SRV-02 — see action act-003.
notes.cl7: Disk image and memory capture sealed in evidence locker E-14; working copies encrypted on evidence NAS.
